variants.notes: Practical local baseline for farmers, labourers, militia, and road workers.
Harder, broader human silhouette for guards, soldiers, and caravan muscle.
Slightly narrower civilian shape for clerks, healers, priests, and scholars.
Taller travel-read silhouette for guides, messengers, and mixed-road NPCs.
Gentler practical civilian read for medics, caretakers, and shrine helpers.
Compact road-and-river merchant shape, sturdy without becoming guard-like.
Older village silhouette with a heavier middle and slower posture.
Narrow messenger or young official read for towns, temples, and errands.
Stocky civilian authority shape for foremen, quartermasters, and storekeepers.
Spare village silhouette for mourners, refugees, and hard-road civilians.
Quiet ritual or apprentice read without drifting into non-human shapes.
Heavy labourer read for muddy, practical town work.
Rural caretaker read for herbalists, birth helpers, and field medics.
Hands-forward town builder silhouette without becoming military.
Public-facing market and contract silhouette with social weight.
Older fortified-town guard read for retired soldiers and gate crews.
Fast courier read: long arms, light frame, visible tail, and grasping feet.
Lean duelist read with sharper brow, strong hands, and balanced tail.
Calmer simian posture for teachers, monks, and disciplined travellers.
Most acrobatic simian read; useful for thieves, performers, and tricksters.
Light scout read for cliffs, rooftops, branches, and narrow passes.
Small careful gatherer shape with nimble hands and a low tail read.
Visible caller silhouette with expressive brow and upright simian posture.
Nimble hands-forward read for pickers, climbers, and delicate tool users.
Highest-climb silhouette for roof workers, scouts, and alarm runners.
Ceremonial listener shape with alert brow, narrow frame, and visible tail.
Hands-forward entertainment and dexterity read, with tail balance visible.
Small gatherer shape that keeps the simian hands, feet, and tail read.
Vertical guide silhouette for cliff paths, exposed roads, and difficult climbs.
Record-keeper shape using face, posture, and hands rather than baked clothing.
Market-roof worker silhouette with long reach and grasping feet.
Hard-travel runner for urgent messages, thin-road commerce, and fast climbs.
Broadest practical craft silhouette.
Defensive military read: short, wide, stable.
Less martial but still stockier than human baseline.
Compressed, heavy, old-soldier silhouette.
Craft-heavy but calmer than smith; barrel torso and strong hands.
Short grounded elder/caretaker silhouette with family authority.
Negotiator or toll keeper: broad presence without full guard bulk.
Lowest heaviest practical labour silhouette; the stocky rule at maximum.
Rounder craft/cellar silhouette while staying short and solid.
Broad defender shape for family guards and shield crews.
Public voice silhouette with weight and authority, not extra height.
Hand-heavy maker silhouette for carvers, butchers, and stone detailers.
Very grounded family-caretaker shape with clan authority.
Maximum civic defender silhouette while still short-heavy.
Negotiator shape with compact mass and visible clan marks.
Deep labour silhouette for stone, road, and heavy hauling crews.
Broad wetland guide shape with grounded feet.
Quiet healer/alchemist read: head and hands carry the silhouette.
Leanest Mirefolk option while keeping broad feet and amphibian head.
Heavier practical silhouette for feared but necessary work.
Calm broad caretaker shape for wetland healers and family roles.
Long-footed runner read for messengers who cross mud and water quickly.
Ritual wetland silhouette with the head and eyes doing most of the read.
Hands-and-feet worker shape for fishers, net menders, and marsh craft.
Still wetland watcher shape with eyes and feet doing the read.
Craft worker read with visible hands and damp reed identity.
Wetland doctor/alchemist silhouette with broad head and careful hands.
Smaller adult Mirefolk read for shy workers and quiet marsh villagers.
Wetland medical silhouette with careful hands and large watchful head.
Long-footed wet-road courier for water margins and soft ground.
Old marsh authority silhouette with broad head and low stance.
Protective wetland watch shape without losing amphibian softness.
Core Ravenfolk read: narrow feathered body, small crest, true beak, and quill marks.
Record-keeper shape: slim, feathered, beaked, with fine quill marks.
The sturdier Ravenfolk shape, still narrow beside human guards.
Most uncanny option: very tall, narrow, crested, and visibly feathered.
Quieter civic Ravenfolk: feather body and beak do the species read.
Physical authority variant; broader for Ravenfolk, not broad for the world.
Smaller grief-ritual silhouette with sharp beak and quiet feather detail.
Tall authority silhouette with crest, beak, and strong feather rows.
Small office silhouette; anatomy remains feather-first.
Tall quiet attendant shape with crest and dark body feathers.
Negotiator shape with a slightly stronger frame and tail-feather read.
Most severe narrow variant: crest, tail feathers, beak, and quill marks.
Narrow record-keeper with beak and feather rows doing the identity work.
Ceremonial witness shape: slender, crested, feathered, and beaked.
Small mourning silhouette with narrow shoulders and tail feathers.
Broadest Ravenfolk guard shape, still feathered and slender beside humans.
Guarded old-growth silhouette; taller and rooted without elf language.
Older stranger read, with bark marks doing more work than leaves.
Softer healer/gardener shape; still plant-linked, not elven.
Heavy, patient, rooted elder silhouette.
Memory-keeper silhouette with bark marks and an older head-heavy read.
Smaller working Rootborn read for gatherers, tenders, and quiet villagers.
Guarded trunk-like silhouette without turning into a stone golem.
Tall thin young-root silhouette for singers, speakers, and spirit-touched NPCs.
Small caretaker of seeds, groves, and stored living things.
Wide sheltering trunk silhouette for guards, elders, and boundary keepers.
Tall walking-root pilgrim shape with moss and patience.
Head-heavy speaker or mediator shape for councils and old-memory roles.
Gentle grove-medicine shape, plantlike but not elven.
Old bark-heavy authority shape for memory, judgment, and grove law.
Boundary guard silhouette with rooted feet and thorn-crown read.
Tall travelling-root shape for roads, rites, and seasonal movement.
